svg.text: Microsoft Agent Framework — Workflow Execution Model
WorkflowBuilder · Directed Graph · Modified Pregel (BSP) · Superstep-based Processing
Workflows are constructed using the WorkflowBuilder class — a declarative, directed graph of Executors connected by Edges
Edges define message routing · Each executor has @handler methods
Messages flow along edges · Types checked at build time
The framework performs comprehensive validation when .build() is called — catches errors before execution
@handler input types must match edge source output
from the start executor via edges
Each executor must have unique id
No self-loops or duplicate add_edge calls
Modified Pregel execution model — Bulk Synchronous Parallel (BSP) approach with superstep-based processing
All triggered executors run in parallel, but the workflow does NOT advance until every executor completes
⚠ Path A's step2 & step3 CANNOT advance
into one executor for true parallel execution
The Bulk Synchronous Parallel model provides critical guarantees for production workflows
Given the same input, the workflow always
executes in the same order — reproducible results
State can be saved at superstep boundaries
for fault tolerance — resume after crash
each sees a consistent view of all messages
async for event in workflow.run(msg, stream=True):
Waits for full completion · Returns all events
Microsoft Agent Framework · Workflow Builder & Execution · Modified Pregel (BSP) · learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework
Related: Executors · Edges · Events · State Management · Agents in Workflows · Checkpointing · Visualization
